pi_items.source: Dataset file downloaded from http://www.learneconometrics.com/gretl/poe5/data/food.gdt and verified as valid Gretl XML dataset
XML parsing of food.gdt shows <gretldata ... endobs="40" ...> and manual verification of all observation entries

pi_items.verified_value: food_exp (food expenditure per week in $), income (weekly income in $100 units)
pi_items.source: XML metadata in food.gdt describes: 1. food_exp: 'household food expenditure per week', 2. income: 'weekly household income'

pi_items.verified_value: food_exp = const + income (simple linear regression with intercept)
pi_items.source: Task description explicitly states model specification

pi_items.verified_value: Dataset is from Principles of Econometrics 5th Edition (Hill, Griffiths, Lim); available at principlesofeconometrics.com
pi_items.source: Web search confirms POE5 food dataset is standard educational resource; dataset downloaded from official Lee Adkins Gretl repository at learneconometrics.com

pi_items.metadata_value: Not explicitly stated in metadata, but verifier.py contains reference values
pi_items.source: Calculated from actual food.gdt dataset using OLS LOO-CV formula: MSPE = mean((y_i - yhat_i)/(1 - h_ii))^2

pi_items.metadata_value: Not explicitly stated in metadata, but verifier.py contains reference values
pi_items.source: Calculated from actual food.gdt dataset using RMSPE = sqrt(MSPE)
Calculated from verifier.py RAW_DATA array (lines 20-31), which contains 40 [food_exp, income] pairs
Calculated from verifier.py RAW_DATA array using same LOO-CV formula

pi_items.metadata_value: Not mentioned; implicit assumption that verifier.py uses actual food.gdt data
pi_items.verified_value: CRITICAL DISCREPANCY: Verifier.py RAW_DATA differs significantly from actual food.gdt
pi_items.source: Direct comparison of 40 observations: only first 5 match, subsequent 35 observations have different values. Example: obs 6: actual=[243.92, 12.98] vs verifier=[243.43, 12.98]; obs 7: actual=[267.43, 14.2] vs verifier=[109.71, 3.15]
Confirmed in task.json metadata and export_result.sh
Confirmed in task.json metadata and export_result.sh

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the real Principles of Econometrics 5th Edition food.gdt dataset with 40 observations of weekly household food expenditure (Y) and weekly income in $100 units (X), from learneconometrics.com. The dataset is used for Leave-One-Out Cross-Validation on the model food_exp = const + income. CRITICAL ISSUE: The verifier.py file hardcodes different data (RAW_DATA array) than the actual food.gdt file. When comparing the 40 observations: only the first 5 match, while observations 6-40 differ significantly. Using actual food.gdt data yields MSPE=8334.76 and RMSPE=91.29, but verifier.py's RAW_DATA yields MSPE=872.62 and RMSPE=29.54. This means any correctly-implemented agent script using the actual food.gdt file will produce different ground truth metrics than what verifier.py expects, likely causing verification failures despite correct implementation.
